items.title: Two Indian men shot dead in Italy while leaving gurdwara

items.title: New Zealand PM Christopher Luxon meets Akal Takht Jathedar, discusses Sikh community contributions
items.summary: During the meeting, New Zealand Prime Minister Christopher Luxon and Giani Kuldip Singh Gargajj discussed ways to promote mutual harmony, understanding, and inclusivity among the Sikh community and other communities residing in New Zealand

items.title: Humanoid robots race past humans in Beijing half-marathon, showing rapid advances
items.summary: The winning robot, developed by Chinese smartphone brand Honor, finished the race in 50 ⁠minutes and 26 seconds, several minutes faster than the half-marathon world record set by Ugandan runner Jacob Kiplimo in Lisbon last month

items.title: Bulgarians head to the polls to elect a parliament for the eighth time in 5 years
items.summary: Since 2021, the nation of 6.5 million has struggled with fragmented parliaments that produced weak governments, none of which managed to survive more than a year before being brought down by street protests or backroom deals in parliament

items.title: Bangladesh raises fuel prices as conflict in West Asia drives up costs
items.summary: Officials ⁠said the increase was unavoidable ⁠as rising crude prices, supply chain disruptions and higher freight and insurance costs pushed up import expenses in ​recent weeks, particularly after ‌oil prices have jumped during the seven-week-old war on Iran.

items.title: Trump vs Pope | Two Americans, two paths

items.title: Watch: Constitution Amendment Bill, part of delimitation package, defeated
items.summary: Major political and geopolitical developments lead today’s headlines. The Constitution Amendment Bill on women’s quota faces a setback in the Lok Sabha, while Sri Lanka’s Power and Energy Minister resigns amid controversy. Iran has reopened the Strait of Hormuz, easing concerns over global shipping, even as remarks by Tejasvi Surya on Telangana trigger a political storm. The Supreme Court also flags illegal sand mining in Madhya Pradesh. Stay tuned for the full breakdown.

items.title: Truce in effect as Trump says Israel ‘prohibited’ from bombing Lebanon
items.summary: Shortly after the truce went into effect at midnight local time, the Army told residents of the south — many of whom had to flee their homes following sweeping Israeli evacuation warnings — not to return, citing “several Israeli acts of aggression”.

items.title: Australian former soldier gets bail on Afghanistan war crime charges

items.title: China calls passage of Japanese warship through Taiwan Strait a 'provocation'
